name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 63 Thursday — TTF €44.13/MWh FLAT (-19.8% 1M, est +42% 3M from pre-crisis €31.20). TTF SURPRISINGLY DECOUPLED FROM BRENT +12-15% 1M — drivers: storage adequate going into spring, US LNG supply firm, mild weather. Cheniere LNG +2.7% Apr 29 close $272.23 (TTF stable + Qatar 17% offline = US LNG export-arb durable). Below €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; ECB has not characterised Hormuz risk as systemic. UAE OPEC exit takes effect TOMORROW May 1 — primary impact is oil not gas; not directly TTF-relevant. Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First makes extended-economic-warfare base case but TTF has structural offsets (storage, US LNG) that Brent doesn't — paper TTF continues to behave more like inflation event than crisis event. Cascade-intensity threshold trigger remains WATCHING at €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): TTF holding €44+ while Brent breached $115 intraday — paper TTF behaving more like inflation event than crisis-event. Cheniere LNG +2.2% Wed close $264.98 (TTF holding €44+ + Qatar capacity 17% offline = US LNG export-arb durable). Three structural drivers persist: (a) Hormuz still functionally closed Day 61; war-risk insurance ~1% AWRP capped by US DFC $40B reinsurance backstop; (b) European inventory refill window tightening into summer (storage at 5-year seasonal low); (c) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. UAE OPEC exit Tue Apr 28 effective May 1 = structural Gulf realignment but not directly TTF-relevant (UAE primary impact is oil, not gas). Below €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; ECB has not characterised Hormuz risk as systemic. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
